paper_metadata.title: CTRL: A Conditional Transformer Language Model for Controllable Generation
paper_metadata.abstract: Large-scale language models show promising text generation capabilities, but users cannot easily control particular aspects of the generated text. We release CTRL, a 1.63 billion-parameter conditional transformer language model, trained to condition on control codes that govern style, content, and task-specific behavior. Control codes were derived from structure that naturally co-occurs with raw text, preserving the advantages of unsupervised learning while providing more explicit control over text generation. These codes also allow CTRL to predict which parts of the training data are most likely given a sequence. This provides a potential method for analyzing large amounts of data via model-based source attribution. We have released multiple full-sized, pretrained versions of CTRL at https://github.com/salesforce/ctrl.

extraction_data.innovation: CTRL introduces a conditional language model that uses control codes to generate text with specific constraints (e.g., ratings, domains, tasks). It learns to control generation by incorporating URL metadata during training, enabling fine-grained control over text generation.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Wikipedia (En, De, Es, Fr), Project Gutenberg, 45 subreddits, OpenWebText, news data
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Not explicitly mentioned; likely assumes fine-tuning on specific tasks with control codes
extraction_data.extension: Control codes allow mixing domains (e.g., mixing political and scientific domains) to generate novel cross-domain text, enabling novel cross-domain behavior.
extraction_data.research_problem: Controllable text generation and large language model generalization
extraction_data.application: Text generation, controllable language modeling, source attribution analysis
extraction_data.paper_title: CTRL: A Conditional Transformer Language Model for Controllable Generation

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces a controllable language model using domain-specific control codes and penalized sampling to balance truthfulness and diversity in generation.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: 140 GB of text from Wikipedia (En, De, Es, Fr), Project Gutenberg, OpenWebText, news data, Amazon Reviews, question-answer pairs (ELI5, MRQA, etc.)
extraction_data.application: Text generation, domain-specific text generation, question answering, review generation
extraction_data.research_problem: Controllable generation of text with domain-specific styles and avoiding repetition.
extraction_data.extension: Penalized sampling to prevent repetition and domain-specific control codes for style control
extraction_data.paper_title: CTRL: A Conditional Transformer Language Model for Controllable Generation
